mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output and corporate profits, which directly boosts equity valuations through improved earnings expectations over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC between monthly INDPRO growth and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 60% of months with INDPRO increase.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in housing starts above 700k units signals a robust recovery in residential investment and consumer wealth, driving positive equity returns as confidence in the housing market strengthens.
failure_condition: In months following the HOUST > 700k trigger, the 20-day SPX return is negative in 50% or more of instances, or the average return is not statistically greater than zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the economy grows (real GDP rising) and the Fed funds rate remains near zero, extraordinarily accommodative monetary policy amplifies the positive impact of demand on equities by compressing discount rates and encouraging risk-taking.
failure_condition: In quarters with positive GDP growth and FEDFUNDS below 0.25%, 60-day SPX returns are positive in fewer than 60% of observations, or the joint signal's Rank IC is below 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: In low-inflation regimes (CPI YoY < 3%), a gradual rise in consumer prices reflects healthy aggregate demand and supports higher equity prices as it allows firms to maintain margins without aggressive rate hikes.
failure_condition: When annual CPI inflation is below 3%, the Rank IC of month-over-month CPI change versus 20-day SPX returns is negative over any 3-year period, or positive returns occur in less than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Large monthly nonfarm payroll gains (>150k) signal strong labor demand and rising household incomes, boosting consumer spending expectations and generating positive short-term equity momentum.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by more than 150,000 in a single month
failure_condition: In the 5 trading days following a payroll increase >150k, the S&P 500 is negative in more than 40% of cases, or the average 5-day return is not significantly above the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment falls to 9% or below concurrently with housing starts exceeding 650k, a combined improvement in labor and housing markets validates a self-reinforcing recovery cycle, supporting equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, 60-day SPX returns are positive in less than 60% of observations, or the joint signal fails to produce a Sharpe ratio above 0.5 on a quarterly basis.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales month-over-month indicate immediate consumption momentum, which translates into positive short-term earnings surprises and a buoyant equity market over the following week.
failure_condition: The Spearman rank correlation between monthly RSAFS growth and 5-day SPX returns is less than 0.05 over any 2-year rolling period, or positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: While the federal funds rate remains at the zero lower bound (≤0.25%), the continuation of highly accommodative monetary policy suppresses the risk-free rate, encouraging equity inflows and supporting rising stock prices.
failure_condition: In periods when FEDFUNDS is 0.25% or below, the 20-day SPX forward return is positive in less than 65% of instances, or the average excess return over the risk-free rate is negative.
